task: Advanced high-value task: Produce an evidence brief and remediation affordability analysis for an IAM attack-path graph using only the supplied embedded packet. Scenario: a senior technical lead must decide whether to hold, release, retest, or redesign based only on supplied evidence for an IAM attack path graph. Embedded packet (do not invent or impute values): Records: R1 admin_edges=21 public_triggers=42 PII_reachable=6; R2 admin_edges=30 public_triggers=38 PII_reachable=9; R3 admin_edges=16 public_triggers=53 PII_reachable=5. Remediation options (do not modify inputs): O1 reduces admin_edges by 12% cost=66,500; O2 reduces public_triggers by 18% cost=87,300; O3 reduces PII_reachable by 25% cost=48,700; max_budget=111,600. Rules and constraints: a public trigger that reaches PII or an admin edge without MFA is critical. Missing values cannot be imputed - all calculations must use only the embedded packet. Required deliverables: IAM_attack_path_3.xlsx and expert_memo.md. Deliverables must include formulas, intermediate values, row-level citations to the embedded packet values, explicit assumptions, sensitivity checks, and a final recommendation table that answers whether the supplied remediation options are relevant, affordable within the budget, and sufficient to change the final decision (hold/release/retest/redesign). Mark any ambiguous items or data gaps as needs_review; do not invent inputs.
